title: Road Rules
content: No car should turn right in the face of oncoming traffic
Give way to traffic coming from your right.
Any car can move to the centre if it has no car coming from its right. (N.B.- such a car will always be 2nd to leave intersection)
Traffic in the same road moves at the same time. N.B.- Applies if none of them is turning right.
At a Round About, give way to traffic already circulating. (Then apply rule 2).
Any car facing a stop or give way sign, is the last car to go.

title: Question Help
content: Fog light are used when there is a heavy mist/bad weather
You can overtake animal drawn vehicle by whichever side is safe
At a round-about you give way to traffic already circulating
Regulatory signs are also called Mandatory signs
At a narrow bridge you consider width
On a straight ahead lane you should not turn at all
When travelling at 60km/h I leave a gap of 4 cars
When travelling at 90km/h I leave a gap of 6 cars
60km/h is the general speed limit in urban areas
A red reflective triangle should be placed at 30–50 behind a broken vehicle
You engage to lower gear when going down a hill
A circle regulates traffic and a triangle warns traffic
A motor-cycle is permitted to carry not more than one passenger
On a bridge one should not overtake
At a flashing amber you give right of way to traffic on your right
Derestriction means the imposed speed limit has been canceled
Direction arrows used in conjunctions with prohibition lines have a regulatory effect
At a corner vehicles are allowed to park about 7,5m from the corner
We use the right foot on accelerator and left on clutch. ABC = accelerator + brakes + clutch
Hand/park brake is used to keep the vehicle stationary
Missing out some gear changes save fuel because it reduces the amount of time you accelerate
When you meet an obstruction on your side of the road you give the right of way to oncoming traffic
We usually look for motorists and cyclist at a junction because they are harder to see
The legal age for a female applicant to drive a heavy vehicle is 18 years
You put on park lights when parked at a lay-by
When parked on the side of the road you use park lights, side lights and tail light
A green light at a robot controlled intersection means go if safe to do so
A seat belt is not necessary when reversing
A restriction sign signifies that I should not exceed the stated speed
Weight is usually associated with weigh bridges
Reflectors are put white-front, red-back and yellow-sides
Diverging lines are one which form two
You may straddle the yellow broken line when overtaking traffic turning right or stop when you want to pull over
Broken yellow line is also called edge line
Cyclists using a road must ride in a single file
In rural areas we give way to traffic already at the intersection
Filling your car with petrol at a filling station one must switch off the engine

title: General Notes
content: The horn/hooter should be used to warn pedestrians or public or other users of danger
We have five road signs in Zimbabwe namely: I. Danger warning II. Regulatory III. Informative IV. Traffic light signals V. Carriage markings
The rule of the road is keep to the left and give way to any vehicle on your right
Never turn right in front of oncoming vehicle
Controlled intersection is one which is controlled by stop or give way sign, robot or police
Uncontrolled is one which is not controlled by stop or give way but, the rule is give way to vehicle on your right
On Zebra variety you STOP then give precedence to pedestrians
Prohibition lines indicates that you should not overtake
Ambulances, fire brigade vehicles have the right of way when sounding their sirens
When travelling at a low speed you use the left lane
Blind spot means a portion not covered by your mirror
Clutch pedal helps to avoid noise when changing gears
Most important aspect in a bus is the passenger
When you run-over a dog in rural areas you stop and try to find the owner
When involved in an accident you report to the police within 24hrs
We indicate when going out at a round-about
In rural areas you give right of way to any vehicle that enters the junction before you
Transverse lines are also called stop lines

title: STOP Sign Notes
content: Stop immediately behind the stop line
I will stop for traffic from all directions
Stop sign means stop whether the road is clear or not
Give precedence to all crossing traffic

title: GIVE WAY Sign Notes
content: Give way to all crossing traffic from any direction
At give way sign you do not necessary have to stop if the road is clear
DERESTRICTION SIGN – found only in the informative

title: Vehicles, Licensing & Miscellaneous
content: To drive a PSV one has to reach 25 years.
Motor cycle does not carry fire-extinguisher
Motor cycle doesn’t have a reverse gear.
A truck towing two trailers has three reflectors.
The correct sequence of a robot light is amber, red, and green.
A heavy vehicle towing two trailers carries two red triangles.
When a heavy vehicle has broken down I will place a red reflective triangle 30m–50m.
When involved in a serious accident I should report immediately to the police within 24hrs.
The overall stopping distance at 120km/h is 130m.
The reaction distance at 60km/h is 8,3m.
If you see that an oncoming vehicle does not dip its light you cast out your eyes slightly to the left.
When you see continuous white line you should not straddle the line or overtake.
I switch on my headlight 5:30pm to 6:00am.
Using rear fog light in clear daylight I will dazzle other drivers.
When driving behind other car I will dip my head lights or put into low beam.

title: Vehicle Classes
content: CLASS 1 — Buses
CLASS 2 — Lorries
CLASS 3 — Motor cycles
CLASS 4 — Light motor vehicles
CLASS 5 — Tractors and Caterpillars
Applicants for classes 1, 2 and 5 must be above the age of 18, while classes 3 and 4 requires the age of 16 years and above. A heavy vehicle is a vehicle that exceeds 2300 kilograms.
Sequence of a robot R.G.A Not a sequence of a robot R.A.G
Correct sequence of a robot G.A.R and A.R.G Not correct sequence A.G.R and G.R.A
